strInstructions: Prepare the empanada dough and let it rest. Cook onions in oil or butter until soft, then add ground beef, paprika, chili powder, cumin, oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook until the beef is browned and the filling is flavorful. Let the filling cool, then mix in chopped hard-boiled eggs and green olives. Fill empanada discs with the beef mixture, fold, seal the edges, and brush with egg wash. Bake until golden brown and serve warm.

Cook and shred the chicken. Sauté onion, garlic, and bell pepper until softened. Add tomatoes, cumin, oregano, paprika, salt, and pepper, then stir in the shredded chicken. Cook until the filling is moist but not watery, then let it cool. Fill empanada discs with the chicken mixture, fold, seal, and brush with egg wash. Bake until the pastry is golden and crisp.

Prepare chimichurri by combining parsley, garlic, oregano, red pepper flakes, red wine vinegar, olive oil, salt, and pepper. Grill Argentine-style chorizo sausages until browned and cooked through. Split the bread rolls and toast them lightly on the grill. Place the grilled chorizo inside each roll and spoon chimichurri generously over the sausage. Serve hot.

Pat the provolone cheese dry and sprinkle it with oregano and red pepper flakes. Heat a small cast-iron skillet or grill-safe pan until very hot. Add the cheese and cook until the bottom forms a golden crust while the center melts. Carefully flip or broil the top until bubbling and browned. Drizzle with olive oil and serve immediately with crusty bread.

Season thin beef cutlets with salt and pepper. Dip each cutlet in beaten eggs mixed with garlic and parsley, then coat with breadcrumbs. Fry in hot oil until golden on both sides. Place the fried milanesas on a baking sheet, top each with tomato sauce, ham, and mozzarella cheese. Bake or broil until the cheese melts and bubbles. Serve with fries, salad, or mashed potatoes.
searchAliases: beef milanesa with ham and cheese

strInstructions: Prepare beef milanesas by coating thin beef cutlets in beaten egg and breadcrumbs, then frying until golden. Split crusty rolls or baguettes and spread with mayonnaise or chimichurri. Add the fried milanesa, lettuce, tomato, onion, ham, cheese, and a fried egg if desired. Close the sandwich and serve immediately.

Finely chop the garlic and red onion in a food processor or by hand. Add parsley, cilantro if using, oregano, lime juice, red wine vinegar, olive oil, salt, and red pepper flakes. Pulse or stir until the herbs are finely chopped but the sauce is still loose and rustic. Let the sauce rest so the flavors blend. Serve with grilled meats, vegetables, empanadas, or bread.

Prepare a charcoal or wood fire and let it burn down to steady embers. Season beef ribs, short ribs, sausages, and other meats simply with coarse salt. Place the larger cuts on the grill first and cook slowly over indirect heat. Add sausages and smaller cuts later. Turn occasionally and cook until the meats are browned, smoky, and cooked to preference. Serve with chimichurri, grilled bread, and salad.

Soak white hominy and beans overnight. In a large pot, cook pork, beef, pancetta, chorizo, hominy, beans, squash, and aromatics with enough water or stock to cover. Simmer slowly until the meats are tender, the hominy and beans are soft, and the squash thickens the stew. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. Prepare a spicy oil with paprika, chili, onion, and fat, then spoon it over the locro before serving.

Brown beef cubes in oil, then remove. Sauté onion, garlic, and bell pepper until softened. Add tomatoes, broth, potatoes, sweet potatoes, corn, squash, dried apricots or peaches, and seasonings. Return the beef to the pot and simmer until the meat and vegetables are tender. Cook until the stew is thick, slightly sweet, and savory. Serve hot.

Lay the flank steak flat and season with salt, pepper, garlic, parsley, and spices. Arrange carrots, bell pepper strips, hard-boiled eggs, and spinach or herbs over the meat. Roll tightly into a log and tie securely with kitchen string. Simmer or roast until the beef is tender. Cool slightly, remove the string, and slice into rounds. Serve warm or cold.

Simmer veal or beef with aromatics until tender, then cool and slice thinly. Prepare the sauce by blending tuna, anchovies, mayonnaise, capers, lemon juice, and a little cooking broth until smooth and creamy. Arrange the sliced meat on a platter and cover with the tuna-caper sauce. Chill before serving and garnish with extra capers.

Prepare pizza dough and let it rise. Divide the dough into two rounds. Place one round in a greased pan and cover generously with mozzarella cheese. Top with the second dough round and seal the edges. Cover with sliced onions, oregano, olive oil, and a little salt. Bake in a hot oven until the crust is golden, the onions are softened, and the cheese inside is melted.

Prepare pizza dough and let it rise until doubled. Stretch the dough into an oiled pan. Top with a generous layer of thinly sliced onions, olive oil, oregano, and salt. Bake in a hot oven until the crust is crisp and golden and the onions are sweet and lightly browned. Serve hot or at room temperature.

Whisk chickpea flour with water, olive oil, salt, and pepper until smooth. Let the batter rest so the flour hydrates. Heat an oiled pan or pizza tray in a hot oven, then pour in the batter. Bake until the edges are crisp and the top is set and lightly golden. Slice and serve with pizza or as a snack.

Remove corn husks carefully and reserve them for wrapping. Grate or process the fresh corn kernels. Sauté onion and bell pepper in butter or oil until soft, then add the grated corn, milk, paprika, basil, salt, and pepper. Cook until thickened. Spoon the corn mixture onto softened corn husks, fold into packets, and tie securely. Steam or simmer the packets until firm and cooked through.

Boil potatoes until tender, then mash with butter, milk, salt, and pepper. Sauté onions and garlic, then add ground beef, paprika, cumin, oregano, salt, and pepper. Cook until the beef is browned. Stir in chopped hard-boiled eggs and olives if desired. Spread the beef filling in a baking dish and cover with mashed potatoes. Bake until the top is golden and the filling is bubbling.

Preheat the oven. In a bowl, combine tapioca starch, baking powder, salt, eggs, butter, milk, and shredded cheese. Mix until a soft dough forms. Shape the dough into small balls and place them on a baking sheet. Bake until puffed, lightly golden, and chewy inside. Serve warm as a snack or breakfast bread.

Cream butter and sugar until light, then add egg yolks, vanilla, lemon zest, and cognac or brandy if using. Mix cornstarch, flour, baking powder, and baking soda separately, then combine with the butter mixture to form a soft dough. Chill the dough, roll it out, and cut into rounds. Bake until pale and set. Cool completely, then sandwich pairs of cookies with dulce de leche and roll the edges in coconut.

Beat dulce de leche and cream cheese until smooth and fluffy. Dip chocolate cookies briefly in coffee or milk to soften them. Arrange a layer of cookies in a dish, then spread with the dulce de leche cream. Repeat layers until the dish is filled, finishing with cream on top. Chill for several hours or overnight until set. Dust with cocoa or grated chocolate before serving.
searchAliases: no bake dulce de leche cake

strInstructions: Cut potatoes into thin matchsticks or use shoestring potatoes. Fry the potatoes until crisp and golden, then drain. In a skillet, sauté onion with a little oil until softened. Add ham and cook briefly. Add the fried potatoes and beaten eggs, then stir gently over low heat until the eggs are just set but still creamy. Season with salt and pepper and serve immediately.

Finely dice the red bell pepper, green bell pepper, onion, tomato, and garlic. Place the vegetables in a bowl. Add olive oil, red wine vinegar, oregano, crushed red pepper, salt, and black pepper. Mix well until everything is coated. Let the salsa rest for at least 20 minutes so the flavors blend. Serve with grilled meats, choripan, empanadas, or bread.

Cut the chicken into serving pieces and season with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a disco, large wok, or deep skillet and brown the chicken on all sides. Remove the chicken and sauté onions, garlic, bell peppers, and carrots until softened. Return the chicken to the pan, then add beer or white wine, tomatoes, broth, and seasonings. Simmer until the chicken is tender. Add peas and cream near the end, then cook briefly until the sauce thickens. Serve hot with bread, rice, or potatoes.

Cook spinach or chard until wilted, then squeeze out excess liquid and chop. Sauté onion and garlic in oil until soft. Mix the greens with ricotta, Parmesan, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Line a pie dish with pastry dough, then add the filling. Make small wells in the filling and crack eggs into them. Cover with the second pastry sheet, seal the edges, and brush with egg wash. Bake until the crust is golden and the filling is set.

Cook diced beef with onions, potatoes, paprika, cumin, chili powder, salt, and pepper until tender and flavorful. Let the filling cool, then stir in chopped hard-boiled eggs, green onions, and olives if using. Place spoonfuls of filling onto empanada discs, fold, and seal with a repulgue edge. Brush with egg wash and bake until golden, or fry until crisp.

Season thin beef tenderloin steaks with salt and pepper. Cook quickly on a hot skillet or grill until browned and cooked to preference. Toast the sandwich rolls. Spread mayonnaise on the bread, then layer lettuce, tomato, the cooked beef, ham, cheese, and a fried egg. Close the sandwich and serve immediately with fries.

Prepare a soft yeast dough with flour, sugar, yeast, milk, eggs, butter, and salt. Let it rise, then roll it out and spread with softened butter or laminated butter. Fold and roll the dough to create layers. Cut into triangles and roll each piece into a crescent shape. Let the medialunas rise again, then brush with egg wash and bake until golden. Brush with sugar syrup while warm for a shiny finish.

Pour milk into a heavy saucepan and add sugar, baking soda, and vanilla. Bring to a gentle simmer over medium heat, stirring to dissolve the sugar. Reduce heat and cook slowly, stirring often, until the milk thickens and turns deep caramel brown. Continue cooking until the mixture reaches a spreadable consistency. Cool before using as a filling, topping, or spread.

Make a soft pastry dough by combining flour, baking powder, sugar, butter, eggs, vanilla, and lemon zest. Chill the dough briefly. Roll most of the dough into a tart pan. Soften quince paste with a little water or wine, then spread it over the crust. Cut the remaining dough into strips and arrange in a lattice pattern over the filling. Bake until golden and set. Cool before slicing.

Whisk flour, eggs, milk, sugar, melted butter, and salt into a smooth thin batter. Let the batter rest briefly. Heat a lightly buttered skillet and pour in a small amount of batter, swirling to coat the pan. Cook until set, then flip and cook the other side. Spread each pancake with dulce de leche, roll or fold, and dust with powdered sugar if desired.
searchAliases: argentine crepes with dulce de leche
strMeasure5: 2 tbsp melted plus more for pan

strInstructions: Boil potatoes until tender, then peel and mash while warm. Let the potatoes cool slightly, then mix with flour, egg, salt, and nutmeg to form a soft dough. Roll into ropes and cut into small pieces. Shape with a fork if desired. Boil the gnocchi in salted water until they float. Serve with tomato sauce, butter, or tuco and grated cheese.

Sauté onion, garlic, carrot, and bell pepper in oil until softened. Add ground beef or beef pieces and brown well. Stir in tomato sauce, crushed tomatoes, oregano, bay leaf, paprika, salt, and pepper. Simmer until the sauce thickens and the meat is tender. Cook tallarines or spaghetti until al dente, then serve with the tuco sauce and grated cheese.

Prepare fresh pasta dough and let it rest. Mix ricotta, mozzarella, ham, Parmesan, egg, salt, pepper, and nutmeg to make the filling. Roll the pasta dough thinly and cut into rounds. Place filling in the center of each round, cover with another round, and seal well. Boil the sorrentinos in salted water until tender. Serve with tomato sauce, cream sauce, or butter and cheese.

Let the sirloin steaks come to room temperature. Season generously with coarse salt and black pepper. Heat a grill or cast-iron pan until very hot. Cook the steaks until deeply browned on the outside and done to preference inside. Rest for several minutes before slicing. Serve with chimichurri, salsa criolla, salad, or fries.

Pat the skirt steak dry and season both sides with coarse salt and pepper. Heat a grill over high heat. Grill the steak quickly until browned and cooked to preference, turning once. Rest for a few minutes, then slice against the grain. Serve with chimichurri sauce and grilled bread or salad.

Prepare a medium grill with steady heat. Place morcilla sausages on the grill and cook gently, turning occasionally, until heated through and lightly crisp on the outside. Avoid piercing the sausages so they do not burst. Serve hot as part of an asado with bread, chimichurri, and salsa criolla.

Cut pork shoulder into large chunks and season with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a disco, wok, or heavy pan and brown the pork well. Add onions, garlic, bell peppers, carrots, and tomatoes, then cook until softened. Pour in beer or white wine and broth. Simmer until the pork becomes tender and the sauce reduces. Serve with potatoes, rice, or bread.

Mix flour, salt, and yeast in a bowl. Add melted beef fat or lard and warm water, then knead until smooth. Let the dough rest until slightly risen. Roll out the dough and cut into small rounds. Prick the tops with a fork, place on a baking sheet, and bake until lightly golden and crisp. Serve with mate, coffee, or tea.

Soak stale bread in milk until softened, then mash well. Mix in eggs, sugar, vanilla, lemon zest, and raisins if using. Make caramel by melting sugar until amber, then pour it into a baking mold. Add the bread mixture over the caramel. Bake in a water bath until set. Cool, chill, and unmold before serving.

Make a firm dough with flour, egg yolks, butter, and a little water. Rest the dough, then divide into thin rounds. Roll each round very thin and bake until crisp. Layer the baked discs with dulce de leche, stacking them into a tall cake. Cover the top with Italian meringue and lightly toast if desired. Chill briefly before slicing.
searchAliases: argentine dulce de leche layer cake

strInstructions: Prepare or use a sponge cake and slice it into layers. Whip cream with sugar until thick. Spread dulce de leche over one cake layer, then add whipped cream, chopped walnuts, meringue pieces, and peaches or chestnuts if using. Add the next cake layer and repeat. Cover the cake with whipped cream and decorate with coconut, meringue, nuts, or powdered sugar. Chill well before serving.

Soak raisins, dried fruit, and nuts with rum, brandy, or tea until softened. Cream butter and sugar, then add eggs one at a time. Mix in flour, baking powder, spices, cocoa or molasses if using, and the soaked fruit mixture. Pour into a lined cake pan and bake slowly until dense, dark, and cooked through. Cool completely before slicing. The cake improves after resting for a few days.

Slice the dulce de batata into thick pieces. Slice a mild firm cheese such as queso fresco, queso cremoso, or fresh mozzarella into similar-sized pieces. Arrange one slice of dulce de batata with one slice of cheese on each plate. Serve chilled or at room temperature as a simple Argentine dessert.
searchAliases: argentine cheese and sweet potato dessert

strInstructions: Cut firm slices of quince paste or sweet potato paste. Cut equal slices of fresh cheese such as queso fresco, queso cremoso, or mozzarella. Place one slice of cheese and one slice of dulce together on a plate. Serve cold or at room temperature as a traditional Argentine dessert.
strIngredient1: Dulce de Membrillo or Dulce de Batata

strInstructions: Prepare a pastry dough with flour, sugar, butter, egg, baking powder, and lemon zest. Chill briefly. Mix ricotta with sugar, eggs, vanilla, and lemon zest until smooth. Line a tart pan with part of the dough, add the ricotta filling, then cover with the remaining dough or a lattice top. Bake until the pastry is golden and the filling is set. Cool before dusting with powdered sugar.

Make caramel by melting sugar in a saucepan until amber, then pour it into a flan mold. Whisk eggs, milk, sugar, and vanilla until smooth. Pour the custard into the caramel-lined mold. Bake in a water bath until just set. Cool completely, then chill for several hours. Unmold and serve with dulce de leche and whipped cream.
searchAliases: argentine flan with dulce de leche

strInstructions: Cut puff pastry into strips and wrap each strip around a small metal cone mold. Brush with egg wash and bake until puffed and golden. Let the pastry cones cool completely, then carefully remove the molds. Fill each cone with dulce de leche using a piping bag. Dust with powdered sugar before serving.

Cream butter and sugar until smooth. Add egg, vanilla, and lemon zest, then mix in flour and baking powder to form a soft dough. Shape the dough into small balls and place on a baking sheet. Press a small indentation in the center of each cookie. Fill with softened quince paste or dulce de membrillo. Bake until lightly golden and set. Cool before serving.

Line a tart or pie pan with one sheet of pastry dough. Mix chopped ham, shredded cheese, eggs, cream or milk, salt, pepper, and nutmeg in a bowl. Pour the filling into the pastry shell. Cover with another pastry sheet or leave open-faced, then seal the edges if covered. Bake until the crust is golden and the filling is set. Cool slightly before slicing.

Boil potatoes and carrots until tender, then dice them. Cook peas until tender and drain well. Let all vegetables cool completely. In a large bowl, combine potatoes, carrots, peas, mayonnaise, salt, and pepper. Mix gently until evenly coated. Chill before serving as a side dish for asado, holidays, or everyday meals.
